items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:
Candice Carty-Williams, Patrick Freyne and Guardian readers discuss the titles they have read over the last month. Join the conversation in the comments

I just finished reading Wimmy Road Boyz by Sufiyaan Salam. I

items.hook: Johann Sebastian Bach leads the first performance of his cantata Erforsche mich, Gott, und erfahre mein Herz, BWV 136, in Leipzig on the eighth Sunday after Trinity.
items.body: Johann Sebastian Bach was a German composer and musician of the late Baroque period. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: How to Get Filthy Rich in Rising Asia is Mohsin Hamid's third novel, published in 2013 by Hamish Hamilton in the United Kingdom and Riverhead Books in the United States.
items.body: Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Coronation of Emperor Pedro II of Brazil.
items.body: Dom Pedro II, known as "the Magnanimous", was the second and final emperor of the Empire of Brazil. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: It is also the first Soilwork release to feature guitarist David Andersson; founding guitarist Peter Wichers left Soilwork for the second time in 2012, making this the second album to not feature him.
items.body: The Living Infinite is the ninth studio album by Swedish melodic death metal band Soilwork, released on 27 February 2013 in Asia, 1 March 2013 in Europe, on 4 March 2013 in the UK and on 5 March 2013 in the US. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: ‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: Leonardo's Swans is an international bestseller by Karen Essex, published by Doubleday in 2006.
items.body: The novel tells the story of the rivalry between the powerful Este sisters, Beatrice and Isabella, princesses of the House of Ferrara, as they competed for the attentions of both the Duke of Milan and Leonardo da Vinci when the artist was court painter in the

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

Brenda Fricker, who became the first female Irish Oscar winner for acting with My Left Foot, has died ag

items.hook: A racially charged incident in a bar sparks the six-day Hough riots in Cleveland, Ohio; 1,700 Ohio National Guard troops intervene to restore order.
items.body: The Hough riots were riots in the predominantly African-American community of Hough in Cleveland, Ohio, United States which took place from July 18 to 23, 1966. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: A London Life is a novella by Henry James, first published in Scribner's Magazine in 1888.
items.body: The plot revolves around a crumbling marriage and its impact on many other people, especially Laura Wing, the sister of the soon-to-be-divorced wife. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: A gunpowder magazine explosion in Birgu, Malta, kills around 200 people.
items.body: On 18 July 1806, approximately 40,000 lb (18,000 kg) of gunpowder stored in a magazine (polverista) in Birgu, Malta, mistakenly detonated. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: American Civil War: Second Battle of Fort Wagner: One of the first formal African American military units, the 54th Massachusetts Volunteer Infantry, supported by several white regiments, attempts an unsuccessful assault on Confederate-held Battery Wagner.
items.body: The American Civil War was a civil war in the United States between the Union and the Confederacy, which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: The Office Wife is a 1930 American pre-Code romantic drama film directed by Lloyd Bacon, released by Warner Bros.
items.body: Pictures, and based on the novel of the same name by Faith Baldwin. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

Almost 14% of Ireland is bog: vast swathes of moss-carpeted land, below which layers of ancient history have

items.hook: The First Vatican Council decrees the dogma of papal infallibility.
items.body: The First Ecumenical Council of the Vatican, commonly known as the First Vatican Council or Vatican I, was the 20th ecumenical council of the Catholic Church, held three centuries after the preceding Council of Trent which was adjourned in 1563. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: He was a United States House of Representatives member, representing Minnesota during the Fifty-second Congress.
items.body: William Henry Harries was an American lawyer and Democratic politician. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Since 2005 she has been working with the Dutch broadcasting organisation NOS where she interprets the morning news.
items.body: Irma Sluis is a Dutch sign language interpreter. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Cicely Disappears is a 1927 mystery novel by the British writer Anthony Cox, written under the pen name of A.
items.body: Cox used a variety of pseudonyms during his career, in this case based on two properties he was associated with in Watford. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
